# ============================================
# Deploy to Azure App Service Pipeline
# ============================================
# PURPOSE: Builds and deploys our .NET API
# directly to Azure App Service (not Docker)
# This is our PRIMARY deployment pipeline
# Runs on every push to master
# ============================================
# FLOW:
# Code pushed → Build → Publish → Deploy to Azure
# Total time: ~2 minutes
# ============================================

name: Deploy to Azure App Service

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master             # Only deploy from master

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: windows-latest   # Windows matches our .NET app

    steps:
      # Step 1 - Get the code
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Step 2 - Install .NET 9 on build server
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '9.0.x'

      # Step 3 - Download NuGet packages
      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore

      # Step 4 - Compile in Release mode
      # Release = optimized for production
      # No debug symbols = smaller, faster
      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build --no-restore --configuration Release

      # Step 5 - Package the app for deployment
      # Creates optimized production files in ./publish folder
      # This is what actually gets uploaded to Azure
      - name: Publish
        run: dotnet publish SmartJobTracker.API/SmartJobTracker.API.csproj --configuration Release --output ./publish

      # Step 6 - Upload to Azure App Service
      # azure/webapps-deploy = Microsoft's official deploy action
      # app-name = your Azure App Service name
      # publish-profile = authentication file downloaded from Azure
      #   stored as GitHub Secret - never committed to repo!
      # package = folder containing our published files
      - name: Deploy to Azure App Service
        uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v2
        with:
          app-name: 'smartjobtracker-api'
          publish-profile: ${{ secrets.AZURE_WEBAPP_PUBLISH_PROFILE }}
          package: ./publish
